summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a high‑energy ’84 show, highlighted by a smoking Help>Slip>Franklin’s, blazing El Paso and Wharf Rat, despite a few slip‑ups.
review: The recording is a clean soundboard source that delivers the bright, jittery vibe of the Dead’s early‑mid‑80s era.  The second set shines with Help>Slip>Franklin’s, an inspired El Paso, and a thunderous Wharf Rat, while the first set offers fun rarities like Iko Iko.  Minor misfires in Slipknot! and a weak Peggy‑O are noted, but they don’t outweigh the overall excitement.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviewers (8/11) rate the show 4‑5 stars and praise both performance and recording; only a few note specific flaws, yielding a strong positive consensus.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp and clear throughout; reviewers repeatedly call the sound “beautiful” or “excellent,” with only occasional brief glitches (e.g., a slip‑up in the Slipknot→Franklin transition).

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid with quirky blues and rarities; the second set erupts into jazzy funk, delivering a memorable Help>Slip>Franklin’s sequence and powerful close with Wharf Rat.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is described as energetic and crisp on many numbers (Help>Slip>Franklin's, Wharf Rat), though a few reviewers note sloppy cues and a haggard tone during Slipknot.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work gets praise for thoughtful phrasing on Sailor>Saint and his occasional vocal moments (standing in front of Bob’s mic).
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s vocals are highlighted as mature on Sailor>Saint and he shares mic duties with Jerry; some reviewers note his contribution to the “rare” Iko Iko moment.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s keyboards receive glowing remarks, especially on Franklin’s and the jazzy funk feel of the second set.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drums are praised for strong drive on Wharf Rat, but a few mis‑cue moments in Help>Slip are mentioned.
